desc: Take a /etc/passwd-formatted string in arg0 (lines of colon-separated fields). Print just the first field (username) of each line, one per line.
Take a multi-line text in arg0 and a substring in arg1. Print how many lines contain the substring.
args:
error: foo
info: bar
error: baz
warn: qux
error: zip
python:
import sys
q=sys.argv[2]
print(sum(1 for l in sys.argv[1].split('\n') if q in l))

desc: Take multi-line text and a positive integer n. Print the last n lines (in original order).
python:
import sys
n=int(sys.argv[2])
lines=sys.argv[1].split('\n')
print('\n'.join(lines[-n:]))

desc: Take multi-line text and a positive integer n. Print the first n lines.
python:
import sys
n=int(sys.argv[2])
print('\n'.join(sys.argv[1].split('\n')[:n]))

source: common shell pattern (sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head)
desc: Take a multi-line string. Print each distinct line and its count formatted '   N line', most-frequent first, ties broken by first-occurrence. One per line.
args:
apple
banana
apple
cherry
banana
apple

desc: Take multi-line text in arg0. Print the count of newlines in the text (0 for empty input).
python:
import sys
print(sys.argv[1].count('\n'))

desc: Take a string in arg0. Print the count of whitespace-separated tokens.
python:
import sys
print(len(sys.argv[1].split()))

desc: Take a newline-separated list of filenames in arg0 and a target extension in arg1 (no leading dot). Print only filenames matching that extension.

Take a CSV-like string (no header, comma-separated). Take a 0-based column index in arg1 and a threshold integer in arg2. Print rows where the integer in that column is strictly greater than the threshold.
Take a multi-line text in arg0 and a positive integer n in arg1. Print the first n characters of each line.
Take multi-line text and a substring. Print lines NOT containing the substring.
args:
error: a
info: b
error: c
warn: d

desc: Take a multi-line text and a substring. Print every occurrence of the substring (one per line) across all lines, preserving order.
args:
foo bar foo
bar foo baz
foo
python:
import sys
q=sys.argv[2]
for l in sys.argv[1].split('\n'):
    i=0
    while True:
        j=l.find(q,i)
        if j<0: break
        print(q); i=j+len(q)

desc: Take a string and extract all email-like tokens (basic pattern: NAME@DOMAIN.TLD where NAME is alphanumeric+dot, DOMAIN is alphanumeric, TLD is 2-6 alpha). Print each email on its own line, in order of appearance.
args: Contact alice@example.com or bob.smith@foo.bar.io for info, also test@x.co

desc: Take a multi-line log where each line ends with '... took Nms' (or contains 'took Nms' for some integer N). Print the largest N seen.
args:
GET /a took 23ms
POST /b took 150ms
GET /c took 7ms
PUT /d took 89ms
python:
import sys, re
vals=[int(m) for m in re.findall(r'took (\d+)ms', sys.argv[1])]
print(max(vals))

desc: Take multi-line text. Print lines in their original order with later duplicates removed.
args:
a
b
a
c
b
d
a

desc: Take three args: text, target string, replacement string. Print text with all non-overlapping occurrences replaced.
python:
import sys
print(sys.argv[1].replace(sys.argv[2], sys.argv[3]))

desc: Take a string. Print it with runs of spaces collapsed to a single space (do not strip leading/trailing).
python:
import sys, re
print(re.sub(r' +', ' ', sys.argv[1]))

desc: Take a non-negative integer byte count. Print 'X.YZ B/KB/MB/GB/TB' choosing the largest unit where the value remains >= 1, with 2 decimals.
python:
import sys
n=float(sys.argv[1])
for u in ['B','KB','MB','GB','TB']:
    if n < 1024 or u=='TB': print(f'{n:.2f} {u}'); break
    n /= 1024

source: ps aux | grep pattern | grep -v grep
desc: Take a multi-line ps-style listing in arg0 and a pattern in arg1. Print lines that contain the pattern AND do not contain the literal string 'grep'.
args:
root 1 systemd
user 234 firefox
user 567 grep python
user 890 python script.py

desc: Take a multi-line input where each line is 'COMMIT_HASH AUTHOR MESSAGE...' (space-separated, message may contain spaces). Print 'AUTHOR: MESSAGE' for each line.
args:
abc123 alice fix: typo
def456 bob feat: new feature
ghi789 carol refactor: simplify
expected:
alice: fix: typo
bob: feat: new feature
carol: refactor: simplify

desc: Take a JSON object string in arg0 and a top-level key in arg1. Print the value at that key. If the value is a string, print without quotes. If it's a number, print as-is. If absent, print 'null'.
Take a CSV string in arg0 (no quoting concerns). Print it with commas replaced by tabs. Output prints '\t' as literal tab characters.
python:
import sys
print(sys.argv[1].replace(',','\t'))

desc: Take a string. Print all http:// and https:// URLs (run of non-whitespace characters), one per line in order. URLs end at first whitespace or end of string.
args: See https://example.com and http://foo.io for docs, plus https://x.y/path

desc: Take multi-line text. For each maximal run of identical consecutive lines, print '   N line' (count right-aligned to width 4 then space then line).
args:
apple
apple
banana
apple
apple
apple
python:
import sys
lines=sys.argv[1].split('\n')
i=0
while i<len(lines):
    j=i
    while j<len(lines) and lines[j]==lines[i]: j+=1
    print(f'{j-i:>4} {lines[i]}')
    i=j

desc: Take a multi-line string with paragraphs separated by one or more BLANK lines. Print each paragraph as a single line with its internal newlines replaced by spaces, paragraphs separated by '---' on its own line. No trailing '---'.
args:
hello
world

foo
bar
baz

last
expected:
hello world
---
foo bar baz
---
last
python:
import sys, re
paras=[p for p in re.split(r'
\s*
', sys.argv[1]) if p.strip()]
oneliners=[' '.join(p.split('
')) for p in paras]
print('
---
'.join(oneliners))

desc: Take a 3-digit octal permission string (e.g. '755'). Print the equivalent symbolic representation 'rwxr-xr-x' (9 chars).
python:
import sys
p=sys.argv[1]
out=''
for c in p:
    n=int(c)
    out += ('r' if n&4 else '-') + ('w' if n&2 else '-') + ('x' if n&1 else '-')
print(out)

desc: Take a string. Print 'yes' if it is a syntactically valid dotted-quad IPv4 (four 0-255 integers separated by dots, no leading zeros except '0' itself), else 'no'.
python:
import sys
p=sys.argv[1].split('.')
ok=len(p)==4 and all(c.isdigit() and 0<=int(c)<=255 and (c=='0' or not c.startswith('0')) for c in p)
print('yes' if ok else 'no')

desc: Take a multi-line text. Print all ISO-format dates (YYYY-MM-DD) found, one per line in order.
args:
log on 2024-01-15: ok
log on 2024-02-30: weird
no date here
2025-12-01 done

desc: Take a space-separated string of shell arguments in arg0 (no quoting concerns, simple whitespace split). Print 'NAME=N' where NAME is each distinct token and N is its count, sorted alphabetically by NAME, space-separated.
python:
import sys
from collections import Counter
c=Counter(sys.argv[1].split())
print(' '.join(f'{k}={c[k]}' for k in sorted(c)))

desc: Take a Python source code string in arg0. Print the names of all top-level (no leading whitespace) `def NAME(` definitions, one per line in order.
